mappings.rationale: Directly addresses the theory of measurement, units and standards of measurement, and the principles by which measuring instruments operate.
Relevant to the quantitative analysis underlying measurement theory and the evaluation of measurement data.
Directly covers optical, radio, and other telescope types listed under instruments for observing phenomena.
Directly covers microscopes including optical, electron, and scanning tunneling types listed in the section's instruments for observation.
Relevant to astronomical observations and the development of telescopes and other observational instruments.
Directly covers navigational techniques and devices, including compasses, sextants, and surveying instruments.
Relevant to spectroscopes, spectrographs, and diffraction-based instruments used in scientific research.
Relevant to mass spectrometers and instruments for measuring ionizing radiation such as Geiger and scintillation counters.
Covers particle accelerators and detectors such as cyclotrons, synchrotrons, and bubble chambers listed in the section's research apparatus.
Relevant to seismographs, gravimeters, and geophysical measurement systems described in the section.
Relevant to meteorological measurement instruments and systems covered in the section's major systems of observation.
Covers instruments for measuring ionizing radiation including Geiger counters and scintillation counters.
Relevant to instruments for measuring light intensity (photometers) and optical measurement devices (polarimeters, refractometers).
Relevant to the historical development of laboratory equipment, chromatographs, and analytical instruments.
Relevant to instrumentation and control systems that operate or actuate control devices automatically.
Covers the historical development of measuring instruments and scientific apparatus used in physical research.

mappings.vsiAuthor: David George Bowers, Emyr Martyn Roberts
mappings.rationale: Relevant to hydrographic charting and oceanographic measurement systems.
Relevant to oceanographic measurement techniques and hydrographic charting covered in the section's major systems.
Relevant to cameras as instruments for recording phenomena, listed explicitly in the section.
Relevant to the mathematical foundations of measurement theory, control systems, and instrumentation systems.
